darius dell here to present our macro
minute for monday february 14th 2022.

starting with markets uh stocks down
this morning commodities down crypto
down dollar up global bond deals down
big movers this morning we had uh nikkei

germany's tax index and the stocks
europe 600 index both off three percent
vix out 13
to just under 31 10-year german boon
yield down nine basis points that's a 30
move on a day every day basis so
obviously a lot of the markets are
concerned about the escalation in and
around ukraine uh we don't necessarily
need to have a call on that but we do
have signals and tools that'll help us
make uh manage the risk appropriately uh
on the macro front uh friday's february
university of michigan consumer
confidence data out of the u.s confirmed
that the fed may have to tighten the us
economy into recession to quash rampant
inflation if they do not we may get one
anyway so uh you get the headline print
uh that ticked down to 61.7 uh again
these are february numbers uh that's the
lowest price we've seen since october of
2011. current conditions down to 68.5
that's the lowest print we've seen since
august of 2009 so just coming out of the
depths of the financial crisis and great
recession uh expectations index that
ticked down to 57.4 in february that's
the lowest price we've seen since
november 2011.
taking it to the chart here uh we got
the next 12-month uh inflation
expectations um number that ticked up to
five uh percent um in in in february
that's the highest point we've seen
since going back to july of 2008.
we also got the current financial
situation index compared with one year
ago so how do consumers feel relative to
where they were a year ago that take
down to 97 in february big big move down
there that's the lowest price we've seen
since going back to november of 2013
that nine
point month over month deceleration
that's the slowest uh uh that's the
sharpest contraction we've seen in this
index since going back to the lockdowns
of april of 2020 and then lastly the
black line here the expected change in
the consumer financial situation in a
year so looking ahead a year that ticked
down
not take down sorry crashed 13 points uh
to 101 in february that's the lowest
print we've seen uh since august of 2011
for that indicator uh that 13 point
month over month decline is the slowest
it's the sharpest contraction we've seen
since august of 2019. so something is
afoot the fed hasn't hiked the single
interest rates the single industry the
fed hasn't sold marry one bond from its
balance sheet hasn't done any
quantitative tightening and the economy
is already looking recessionary from the
perspective of consumer confidence and
this is something we've been talking
about for months now and we're starting
to see it really show up in the data um
so just keep that uh front and center as
we progress throughout this year a lot
of risk uh from the probable range
perspective uh it's got silver
overbought nothing oversold in our

is neutral vans minus two point nine
